title: Proposal: Move skopeo's experimental-image-proxy into container-libs
body:
Skopeo's experimental-image-proxy command (~1000 lines in cmd/skopeo/proxy.go) exposes a JSON+fd-passing protocol over a Unix socket pair, allowing non-Go programs to fetch container images.  The primary consumer is https://github.com/containers/containers-image-proxy-rs.

The proxy is almost entirely a thin wrapper around containers/image APIs.

The proposed approach is to extract the proxy handler into a new package under container-libs so that it can be reused by other tools.

 Once this proxy library is in place, https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/651 can be reworked to use it instead of introducing its own JSON-RPC server.

@mtrmac @cgwalters PTAL

comments.body:
I think it’s not _quite_ that simple, because the proxy consumes various CLI options that exist only in Skopeo.

So I think the package would probably need to be split into infrastructure (some package in container-libs) + operation handlers (perhaps remaining in Skopeo) + top-level setup (in Skopeo).

But then there’s things like https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1844#discussion_r1066221998 … the argument parsing approach in the proxy is rather too manual, probably unnecessarily (and that is from Go 1.17 times, we might be able to do better with generics) … and … that sort of brings us back to last month’s “should we use a pre-existing RPC?” discussions.

Also, from a RPM packaging point of view, we are not currently set up to package executables out of container-libs. That’s a technically trivial thing but the process to change that would be somewhat costly.

> I think it’s not _quite_ that simple, because the proxy consumes various CLI options that exist only in Skopeo.
> 
> So I think the package would probably need to be split into infrastructure (some package in container-libs) + operation handlers (perhaps remaining in Skopeo) + top-level setup (in Skopeo).
> 
> But then there’s things like [containers/skopeo#1844 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1844#discussion_r1066221998) … the argument parsing approach in the proxy is rather too manual, probably unnecessarily (and that is from Go 1.17 times, we might be able to do better with generics) … and … that sort of brings us back to last month’s “should we use a pre-existing RPC?” discussions.

yes, it won't be a simple `mv` but we need to split the library functionalities from the skopeo plumbing.  The functionality can be still be exposed through skopeo using the same experimental proxy.



> Also, from a RPM packaging point of view, we are not currently set up to package executables out of container-libs. That’s a technically trivial thing but the process to change that would be somewhat costly.

I don't think we need to do this.  Just moving the library functionalities from skopeo to container-libs

> But then there’s things like https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1844#discussion_r1066221998 … the argument parsing approach in the proxy is rather too manual, probably unnecessarily (and that is from Go 1.17 times, we might be able to do better with generics) … and … that sort of brings us back to last month’s “should we use a pre-existing RPC?” discussions.

I think [jsonrpc-fdpass](https://github.com/bootc-dev/jsonrpc-fdpass) addresses both of those points. There's already well-understood tooling used for json-rpc, and the -fdpass is IMO a very small scoped extension to it.

In my defense, json-rpc was kind of less widely used at the time of the proxy introduction, I think [LSP](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/) was the main user, but since then there's [MCP](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/getting-started/intro) and now [ACP](https://agentclientprotocol.com/get-started/introduction).
Yeah...a side goal of having the core functionality in container-libs is we can expose it from podman too, meaning bootc would switch to requiring podman || skopeo, not both.
Procedurally would it help if people from the bootc team were added as reviewers/maintainers for that section of code?
